task: I need to send a short note to a vendor about a repeat issue, and I want the message to be clear enough that there’s no room for confusion about what needs to happen next. Can you help me draft something I could use as a starting point?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a short, direct version:

> We’ve identified a repeat issue with [item / batch / PO]. This has occurred more than once and is affecting [specific field / quantity / spec]. Please review your records, confirm the cause in writing, and provide the corrective action or replacement plan. Please also include the specific record, lot, or transaction reference supporting your conclusion.

If you want, I can make it:
- firmer,
- more neutral,
- or shorter for email.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but it’s still a bit generic. I’d want it tighter and less polite, with the next step spelled out more explicitly. Something like:

We’re seeing a repeat issue with [item / batch / PO]. This is not an isolated event. Please review your records, identify the root cause, and send your corrective action and replacement plan in writing by [date]. Include the lot, transaction, or other specific reference supporting your finding.

Short, direct, no wiggle room. If you want, make it even more blunt.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, blunt version:

> We’re seeing a repeat issue with [item / batch / PO]. This is not an isolated event. Please review your records, identify the root cause, and send your corrective action and replacement plan in writing by [date]. Include the lot, transaction, or other specific reference supporting your finding.

If you want it even harder-edged:

> We’re seeing a repeat issue with [item / batch / PO]. This is a recurring discrepancy, not a one-off. Please review your records, identify the root cause, and send your corrective action and replacement plan in writing by [date]. Include the specific lot, transaction, or record reference supporting your conclusion.

user_simulator.text: Fine. That’s usable. The second version is a little better — “recurring discrepancy, not a one-off” reads cleaner than “not an isolated event.”
